title: arenaria serpens var. palustris
intro: Arenaria serpens var. palustris, also known as marsh sandwort, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of marsh sandwort include its ability to treat various conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. Marsh sandwort is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
